ksa_text: Skill in integrating and synthesizing data from multiple sources and tools, including data fusion, cross-source correlation, and producing coherent analytical findings from heterogeneous datasets.

dcwf.top_element_label: Ability to interpret and incorporate data from multiple tool sources.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of data aggregation tools and techniques

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of data aggregation tools and techniques
Ability to interpret and incorporate data from multiple tool sources.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Applied Analytics & BI KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.437) to justify inclusion for the Director of Data Governance. Without stronger framework alignment, this skill KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain skill KSA be expected in real-world Director of Data Governance roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Job market analysis and framework role definitions confirm Applied Analytics & BI is not a standard competency expectation for Director of Data Governance. Edge-case exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence does not support including this edge-domain KSA in the Director of Data Governance competency profile. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Insufficient signal (score 0.437) and edge-domain placement (Applied Analytics & BI) do not meet inclusion threshold for the Director of Data Governance. All passes support exclusion.
